incident.name: Garden Grove MMA Tank Leak
incident.status_headline: ~50,000 residents remain under mandatory evacuation orders in Garden Grove as OCFA conducts an overnight confirmation mission after a single crack was discovered in the overheated MMA tank at GKN Aerospace; the tank's internal temperature has exceeded the 100Â°F gauge maximum and no injuries have been reported.

evacuation.boundary_text: N: Ball Road, S: Trask Avenue, W: Valley View Street, E: Dale Street â€” includes portions of Garden Grove, Anaheim, Cypress, Stanton, Buena Park, and Westminster

official_statements.text: OCFA Interim Chief TJ McGovern, on crack discovery: 'We're not there yet, but this was a step in the right direction.'
OCFA Capt. Brian Yau: 'There is no vapors being detected by the air monitors, and there is no active leak that is on the ground.'
OCFA Interim Chief TJ McGovern: 'The BLEVE threat is the worst-case catastrophic event that we have been talking about. We are not there yet. We need to run this operation tonight.'

OCFA Division Chief Craig Covey: 'There are literally two options left remaining: One, the tank fails and spills a total of about 6,000 to 7,000 gallons of very bad chemicals into the parking lot and that area. Or two, the tank goes into a thermal runaway and blows up, affecting the tanks that are around it that have fuel or chemicals in them as well.'
